triageFlags.title: Excessive Loan-to-Value Ratio
triageFlags.description: The Loan-to-Value ratio significantly exceeds the maximum allowable limit for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the proposal as the LTV ratio is not compliant with policy.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the maximum LTV for Tier C applicants is 95%. The current LTV of 224.44% is well above this threshold, indicating a high risk of over-leverage.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Threshold
triageFlags.description: The Debt-to-Income ratio exceeds the maximum allowable limit for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the proposal as the DTI ratio is not compliant with policy.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum DTI of 35% for Tier C applicants. The current DTI of 55% indicates that the applicant's debt obligations are disproportionately high relative to their income, posing a significant risk of default.

content.reasoning: The applicant's credit score places them in Tier C, which imposes stricter requirements. The Loan-to-Value ratio and Debt-to-Income ratio both exceed the maximum allowable limits for this tier, indicating a high risk of default.
